findings.remediation: Remove and rotate the secret, then read it from an environment variable.

findings.evidence: [DOC] Placeholder token appears in documentation example.
findings.remediation: Keep placeholder syntax and avoid real tokens in examples.

findings.evidence: [CONFIG] Hook command references a parent directory traversal.
findings.remediation: Resolve paths under the plugin root and reject parent directory traversal.

findings.evidence: [CONFIG] Hook path hardcodes a user-specific absolute path.
findings.remediation: Use `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` or a relative plugin-root path.

findings.evidence: [CONFIG] Hook command evaluates unquoted user-controlled input.
findings.remediation: Remove eval and pass arguments through a quoted array or validated parser.

findings.evidence: [CODE] Credential file is present inside the plugin root.
findings.remediation: Remove the file from the plugin and load credentials from external secret storage.

findings.evidence: [CODE] Script downloads executable content without checksum or signature verification.
findings.remediation: Pin the download, verify checksum or signature, and fail closed on mismatch.

false_positive_notes.reason: Placeholder token is documentation, not a live credential.

remediation_plan.action: Rotate the leaked key and remove it from plugin metadata.
Replace traversal with plugin-root-bounded path resolution.
Remove eval and quote user-controlled arguments.
Remove credential file and purge it from distributed plugin artifacts.
Add checksum or signature verification for downloads.

coverage.scan_scope: plugin root only; symlink targets outside root are not followed
